query: I have a 10-class severity classifier. Class 0 makes up roughly 70 percent of the data, classes 1-6 each make up about 4 percent, and classes 7, 8, 9 each make up about 1 percent. I'm currently reporting 91 percent overall accuracy and 0.62 macro-F1. Is the classifier good?
expected_behavior: Refuses to call 91 percent accuracy 'good' and explicitly identifies that the majority class (~70 percent) makes overall accuracy misleading — always-predict-class-0 already gets ~70 percent

Flags that macro-F1 of 0.62 likely hides per-class collapse on the rare classes (7, 8, 9 — 1 percent each) and demands per-class F1 / per-class recall for those rare classes before declaring the classifier good

Either (a) recommends switching the lead metric to weighted-F1 if deployment cost follows class frequency, OR (b) keeps macro-F1 as lead but requires explicit reporting and justification of per-class performance on the rare classes
